title: onobrychis gracilis subsp. bulgarica
intro: Onobrychis gracilis subsp. bulgarica, also known as Bulgarian sainfoin, is a perennial legume plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to Bulgaria. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Bulgarian sainfoin include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Bulgarian sainfoin is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also consumed as a fresh or dried plant.
